paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Wildfires are becoming an increasing menace in the western United States, with Southern California being the hardest hit area. There's a reason fire squads battling more frequent blazes in Southern California are having such difficulty containing the flames, despite better preparedness than ever and decades of experience fighting fires fanned by the 'Santa Ana Winds'. The wildfires themselves, experts say, are generally hotter, faster, and spread more erratically than in the past.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Megafires, also called 'siege fires', are the increasingly frequent blazes that burn 500,000 acres or more - 10 times the size of the average forest fire of 20 years ago. Some recent wildfires are among the biggest ever in California in terms of acreage burned, according to state figures and news reports.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One explanation for the trend to more superhot fires is that the region, which usually has dry summers, has had significantly below normal precipitation in many recent years. Another reason, experts say, is related to the century-long policy of the US Forest Service to stop wildfires as quickly as possible. The unintentional consequence has been to halt the natural eradication of underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Three other factors contribute to the trend, they add. First is climate change, marked by a 1-degree Fahrenheit rise in average yearly temperature across the western states. Second is fire seasons that on average are 78 days longer than they were 20 years ago. Third is increased construction of homes in wooded areas.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: 'We are increasingly building our homes in fire-prone ecosystems,' says Dominik Kulakowski, adjunct professor of biology at Clark University Graduate School of Geography in Worcester, Massachusetts. 'Doing that in many of the forests of the western US is like building homes on the side of an active volcano.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In California, where population growth has averaged more than 600,000 a year for at least a decade, more residential housing is being built. 'What once was open space is now residential homes providing fuel to make fires burn with greater intensity,' says Terry McHale of the California Department of Forestry firefighters' union. 'With so much dryness, so many communities to catch fire, so many fronts to fight, it becomes an almost incredible job.'

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: That said, many experts give California high marks for making progress on preparedness in recent years, after some of the largest fires in state history scorched thousands of acres, burned thousands of homes, and killed numerous people. Stung in the past by criticism of bungling that allowed fires to spread when they might have been contained, personnel are meeting the peculiar challenges of neighborhood- and canyon-hopping fires better than previously, observers say.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: State promises to provide more up-to-date engines, planes, and helicopters to fight fires have been fulfilled. Firefighters' unions that in the past complained of dilapidated equipment, old fire engines, and insufficient blueprints for fire safety are now praising the state's commitment, noting that funding for firefighting has increased, despite huge cuts in many other programs. 'We are pleased that the current state administration has been very proactive in its support of us, and [has] come through with budgetary support of the infrastructure needs we have long sought,' says Mr. McHale of the firefighters' union.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Besides providing money to upgrade the fire engines that must traverse the mammoth state and wind along serpentine canyon roads, the state has invested in better command-and-control facilities as well as in the strategies to run them. 'In the fire sieges of earlier years, we found that other jurisdictions and states were willing to offer mutual-aid help, but we were not able to communicate adequately with them,' says Kim Zagaris, chief of the state's Office of Emergency Services Fire and Rescue Branch. After a commission examined and revamped communications procedures, the statewide response 'has become far more professional and responsive,' he says. There is a sense among both government officials and residents that the speed, dedication, and coordination of firefighters from several states and jurisdictions are resulting in greater efficiency than in past 'siege fire' situations.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: In recent years, the Southern California region has improved building codes, evacuation procedures, and procurement of new technology. 'I am extraordinarily impressed by the improvements we have witnessed,' says Randy Jacobs, a Southern California based lawyer who has had to evacuate both his home and business to escape wildfires. 'Notwithstanding all the damage that will continue to be caused by wildfires, we will no longer suffer the loss of life endured in the past because of the fire prevention and firefighting measures that have been put in place,' he says.

groups.title: Wildfires
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect:
• Characteristics of wildfires and wildfire conditions today compared to the past:
    - occurrence: more frequent
    - temperature: hotter
    - speed: faster
    - movement: $1383$more unpredictably
    - size of fires: $1384$greater on average than two decades ago
• Reasons wildfires cause more damage today compared to the past:
    - rainfall: $1385$average
    - more brush to act as $1386$
    - increase in yearly temperature
    - extended fire$1387$
    - more building of$1388$in vulnerable places

questions.analysis: 题干中的“movement”对应原文中的“spread”，“more unpredictably”对应原文中的“more erratically”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案所在的句子是“The wildfires themselves, experts say, are generally hotter, faster, and spread more erratically than in the past.”。因此，正确答案是“spread”。

central_sentences.sentence: The wildfires themselves, experts say, are generally hotter, faster, and spread more erratically than in the past.
central_sentences.translation: 专家表示，野火本身通常比过去更热、更快，而且蔓延得更加不规则。

target_sen.target_sen: The wildfires themselves, experts say, are generally hotter, faster, and spread more erratically than in the past.
The wildfires themselves, experts say, are generally hotter, faster, and spread more erratically than in the past.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“size of fires”对应原文中的“size”，“two decades ago”对应“20 years ago”，“greater on average”对应“10 times the size”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案是“10 times”或“ten times”。原文明确指出，当前的火灾规模是20年前平均火灾规模的10倍，因此这个答案是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Megafires, also called 'siege fires', are the increasingly frequent blazes that burn 500,000 acres or more - 10 times the size of the average forest fire of 20 years ago.
central_sentences.translation: 大型火灾，也称为"围攻式火灾"，是越来越频繁发生的燃烧面积达50万英亩或更多的火灾 - 是20年前平均森林火灾规模的10倍。

syn_ary.synonym: size of the average forest fire

target_sen.target_sen: Megafires, also called 'siege fires', are the increasingly frequent blazes that burn 500,000 acres or more - 10 times the size of the average forest fire of 20 years ago.
Megafires, also called 'siege fires', are the increasingly frequent blazes that burn 500,000 acres or more - 10 times the size of the average forest fire of 20 years ago.

questions.analysis: 题目要求填空的部分是关于降雨量（rainfall）和平均值（average）。在原文中，"precipitation"是"rainfall"的同义词，而"normal"是"average"的同义词。原文中提到"significantly below normal precipitation"，其中"below normal"表示低于正常水平，即低于平均值。因此，正确答案是"below"。

central_sentences.sentence: One explanation for the trend to more superhot fires is that the region, which usually has dry summers, has had significantly below normal precipitation in many recent years.
central_sentences.translation: 造成超级热火势趋势的一个解释是，这个地区通常夏季干燥，而且近年来降水量明显低于正常水平。

target_sen.target_sen: One explanation for the trend to more superhot fires is that the region, which usually has dry summers, has had significantly below normal precipitation in many recent years.
One explanation for the trend to more superhot fires is that the region, which usually has dry summers, has had significantly below normal precipitation in many recent years.

questions.analysis: 答案是“fuel”，因为题干中的“more brush to act as”对应文章中的“underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires”。“brush”在文章中被替换为“underbrush”，“act as”在文章中被替换为“primary”，而“fuel”是文章中明确指出的灌木丛的作用。因此，正确答案是“fuel”。

central_sentences.sentence: The unintentional consequence has been to halt the natural eradication of underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires.
central_sentences.translation: 这一无意的结果导致了灌木丛天然清除过程的停止，现在这些灌木丛成为了特大火灾的主要燃料。

target_sen.target_sen: long policy of the US Forest Service to stop wildfires as quickly as possible. The unintentional consequence has been to halt the natural eradication of underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires.

long policy of the US Forest Service to stop wildfires as quickly as possible. The unintentional consequence has been to halt the natural eradication of underbrush, now the primary fuel for megafires.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“extended fire”对应文章中的“fire seasons that on average are 78 days longer”，其中“extended”对应“longer”，“fire”对应“fire”。因此，答案是“seasons”。

central_sentences.sentence: Second is fire seasons that on average are 78 days longer than they were 20 years ago.
central_sentences.translation: 第二个因素是火灾季节，平均比20年前延长了78天。

target_sen.target_sen: Second is fire seasons that on average are 78 days longer than they were 20 years ago.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“more building”对应原文中的“increasingly building”，“vulnerable places”对应“fire-prone ecosystems”。原文句子明确指出“increasingly building our homes in fire-prone ecosystems”，即在易燃生态系统中越来越多地建造房屋。因此，答案是“homes”或“housing”。

central_sentences.sentence: We are increasingly building our homes in fire-prone ecosystems,' 1 says Dominik Kulakowski, adjunct professor of biology at Clark University Graduate School of Geography in Worcester, Massachusetts. 1
central_sentences.translation: 伍斯特马萨诸塞州克拉克大学地理学研究生院生物学兼职教授多米尼克·库拉科夫斯基说："我们正在越来越多地在易发生火灾的生态系统中建造房屋。"

target_sen.target_sen: 'We are increasingly building our homes in fire-prone ecosystems,' 1 says Dominik Kulakowski, adjunct professor of biology at Clark University Graduate School of Geography in Worcester, Massachusetts.

'We are increasingly building our homes in fire-prone ecosystems,' 1 says Dominik Kulakowski, adjunct professor of biology at Clark University Graduate School of Geography in Worcester, Massachusetts.

groups.desc:
Questions 7-13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The amount of open space in California has diminished over the last ten years.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "The amount of open space in California has diminished over the last ten years" 可以通过文章中的句子 "What once was open space is now residential homes" 和 "for at least a decade" 找到对应关系。题干中的 "open space" 对应文章中的 "open space"，"diminished" 对应 "is now residential homes"，"over the last ten years" 对应 "for at least a decade"。因此，题干信息在文章中得到了直接的支持，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: In California, where population growth has averaged more than 600,000 a year for at least a decade, more residential housing is being built.'What once was open space is now residential homes providing fuel to make fires burn with greater intensity,' says Terry McHale of the California Department of Forestry firefighters'union. 'With
central_sentences.translation: 在加利福尼亚州，过去至少十年人口年均增长超过60万，更多的住宅正在建设。加利福尼亚州森林消防员工会的Terry McHale说："曾经的开放空间现在变成了住宅，为火势提供了更强烈的燃料。"

target_sen.target_sen: 'What once was open space is now residential homes providing fuel to make fires burn with greater intensity,' says Terry McHale of the California Department of Forestry firefighters'union.
'What once was open space is now residential homes providing fuel to make fires burn with greater intensity,' says Terry McHale of the California Department of Forestry firefighters'union.
In California, where population growth has averaged more than 600,000 a year for at least a decade, more residential housing is being built.

questions.title: Many experts believe California has made little progress in readying itself to fight fires.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“Many experts believe California has made little progress in readying itself to fight fires”，意思是“许多专家认为加州在准备应对火灾方面几乎没有取得进展”。而原文中明确提到“many experts give California high marks for making progress on preparedness in recent years”，意思是“许多专家对加州近年来在准备应对火灾方面取得的进展给予高度评价”。这与题干中的陈述完全相反，因此正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: That said, many experts give California high marks for making progress on preparedness in recent years, after some of the largest fires in state history scorched thousands of acres, burned thousands of homes, and killed numerous people.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管如此，许多专家对加利福尼亚州近年来在应对准备方面取得的进展给予高度评价，这是在该州历史上一些最大规模的火灾烧毁了数千英亩土地、数千所房屋并造成众多人员伤亡之后。

target_sen.target_sen: That said, many experts give California high marks for making progress on preparedness in recent years, after some of the largest fires in state history scorched thousands of acres, burned thousands of homes, and killed numerous people.

That said, many experts give California high marks for making progress on preparedness in recent years, after some of the largest fires in state history scorched thousands of acres, burned thousands of homes, and killed numerous people.

questions.title: Personnel in the past have been criticised for mishandling fire containment.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Personnel in the past have been criticised for mishandling fire containment" 可以在文章中找到对应的表达。文章中提到 "Stung in the past by criticism of bungling that allowed fires to spread when they might have been contained"，这与题干中的 "Personnel in the past have been criticised for mishandling fire containment" 是同义替换。因此，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Stung in the past by criticism of bungling that allowed fires to spread when they might have been contained, personnel are meeting the peculiar challenges of neighborhood - and canyon- hopping fires better than previously, observers say
central_sentences.translation: 观察人士说，在过去，由于对消防人员处理不当导致火势蔓延（本可以被控制住）的批评，消防人员现在正在更好地应对邻里和峡谷间火势蔓延的特殊挑战。

target_sen.target_sen: Stung in the past by criticism of bungling that allowed fires to spread when they might have been contained, personnel are meeting the peculiar challenges of neighborhood - and canyon- hopping fires better than previously, observers say.

Stung in the past by criticism of bungling that allowed fires to spread when they might have been contained, personnel are meeting the peculiar challenges of neighborhood - and canyon- hopping fires better than previously, observers say.

questions.title: California has replaced a range of firefighting tools
questions.analysis: 题干中的“replaced”在原文中对应的同义词是“up-to-date”和“upgrade”，而“firefighting tools”对应的是“engines, planes, and helicopters”。原文明确提到加州已经兑现了提供更新的消防工具的承诺，因此题干信息在文章中是存在的，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: State promises to provide more up-to-date engines, planes, and helicopters to fight fires have been fulfilled.
central_sentences.translation: 州政府承诺提供更先进的消防车、飞机和直升机来灭火的承诺已经兑现。

central_sentences.sentence: Besides providing money to upgrade the fire engines that must traverse the mammoth state and wind along serpentine canyon roads, the state has invested in better command-and-control facilities as well as in the strategies to run them.
central_sentences.translation: 除了提供资金升级必须穿越这个庞大州并沿着蜿蜒的峡谷道路行驶的消防车外，州政府还投资了更好的指挥控制设施以及运行这些设施的策略。

target_sen.target_sen: State promises to provide more up-to-date engines, planes, and helicopters to fight fires have been fulfilled.

Besides providing money to upgrade the fire engines that must traverse the mammoth state and wind along serpentine canyon roads, the state has invested in better command-and-control facilities as well as in the strategies to run them.'

Firefighters' unions that in the past complained of dilapidated equipment, old fire engines, and insufficient blueprints for fire safety are now praising the state's commitment, noting that funding for firefighting has increased, despite huge cuts in many other programs. '

Besides providing money to upgrade the fire engines that must traverse the mammoth state and wind along serpentine canyon roads, the state has invested in better command-and-control facilities as well as in the strategies to run them.'

questions.title: More firefighters have been hired to improve fire-fighting capacity.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“More firefighters”和“hired”在原文中并没有直接提到。虽然文章提到了改进设备和增加资金支持，但并没有明确提到雇用了更多的消防员。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供相关信息。

central_sentences.reason: 只提装备与资金，未提增聘消防员。
central_sentences.analysis: 文中无“hired more firefighters”信息，故NG。
sentences: State promises to provide more up-to-date engines, planes, and helicopters to fight fires have been fulfilled.

Firefighters' unions that in the past complained of dilapidated equipment, old fire engines, and insufficient blueprints for fire safety are now praising the state's commitment, noting that funding for firefighting has increased, despite huge cuts in many other programs.

central_sentences.sentence: State promises to provide more up-to-date engines, planes, and helicopters to fight fires have been fulfilled.

Firefighters' unions that in the past complained of dilapidated equipment, old fire engines, and insufficient blueprints for fire safety are now praising the state's commitment, noting that funding for firefighting has increased, despite huge cuts in many other programs.

questions.title: Citizens and government groups disapprove of the efforts of different states and agencies working together.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“disapprove”表示不赞成，而原文中提到“impressed”、“praising”、“pleased”等词语，表明人们对不同州和机构合作的努力是赞赏的。此外，原文明确提到“coordination of firefighters from several states and jurisdictions are resulting in greater efficiency”，这与题干中的“efforts of different states and agencies working together”相对应，并且是正面的评价。因此，题干信息与原文信息相反，答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: There is a sense among both government officials and residents that the speed, dedication, and coordination of firefighters from several states and jurisdictions are resulting in greater efficiency than in past 'siege fire' situations.
central_sentences.translation: 政府官员和居民都感觉到，来自多个州和辖区的消防员的速度、奉献精神和协调能力，比过去的"围攻式火灾"情况下更加高效。

target_sen.target_sen: There is a sense among both government officials and residents that the speed, dedication, and coordination of firefighters from several states and jurisdictions are resulting in greater efficiency than in past 'siege fire' situations.

questions.title: Randy Jacobs believes that loss of life from fires will continue at the same levels, despite changes made.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“Randy Jacobs 认为尽管做出了改变，火灾造成的生命损失仍将保持在相同水平”。而原文中Randy Jacobs 的观点是“由于已经实施的防火和灭火措施，我们将不再遭受过去的生命损失”。这表明Randy Jacobs 认为生命损失将减少，而不是保持在相同水平。因此，题干中的信息与文章内容相反，正确答案为 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Notwithstanding all the damage that will continue to be caused by wildfires, we will no longer suffer the loss of life endured in the past because of the fire prevention and firefighting measures that have been put in place,' he says
central_sentences.translation: 他说："尽管野火仍将继续造成损害，但由于已经实施的防火和灭火措施，我们将不再遭受过去所经历的生命损失。"

target_sen.target_sen: I am extraordinarily impressed by the improvements we have witnessed,' says Randy Jacobs, a Southern California based lawyer who has had to evacuate both his home and business to escape wildfires.'

Notwithstanding all the damage that will continue to be caused by wildfires, we will no longer suffer the loss of life endured in the past because of the fire prevention and firefighting measures that have been put in place,' he says.

Notwithstanding all the damage that will continue to be caused by wildfires, we will no longer suffer the loss of life endured in the past because of the fire prevention and firefighting measures that have been put in place,' he says.

content.title: Second nature
content.summary: {'A': '心理学家长期以来认为一个人的性格无法发生有意义的转变，个性的关键特质在很小的年龄就已经确定。然而，研究人员开始更加密切地研究我们可以改变的方式。积极心理学家已经确定了24种我们所钦佩的品质，比如忠诚和善良，并正在研究它们，以找出为什么它们对某些人来说是如此自然的。他们发现，许多这些品质实际上是决定我们对世界的反应方式的习惯行为。好消息是，所有这些都可以学会。一些品质比其他品质更容易发展，其中乐观主义就是其中之一。然而，培养品质需要掌握各种多样且有时令人惊讶的技能。例如，要在生活中带来更多的快乐和激情，你必须敞开心扉去体验负面情绪。培养这样的品质将帮助你实现自己的全部潜力。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了大部分个性特质是可以改变的，通过密歇根大学心理学教授克里斯托弗·彼得森的自身经历作为例证，他从内向变得外向并学会在课堂上更加活跃。', 'C': '大卫·法杰恩鲍姆在大学准备阶段遭遇事故，结束了他的运动生涯，但他发现学校除了普通的咨询外，没有为正在康复和遭受抑郁症的学生提供服务，因此他发起了一个支持小组来帮助其他处于类似情况的人，尽管自己痛苦，但他采取了行动，这是一个乐观主义者的典型反应。', 'D': '苏珊娜·塞格斯特罗姆是肯塔基大学心理学教授，她认为增加乐观主义的关键是培养乐观行为，而不是积极思考。她建议每天写下三件积极的事情，让自己注意好运，这将帮助你相信有利的结果实际上经常发生，从而更容易开始采取行动。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了Tanya Streeter对自由潜水的热情以及她在这项运动中所取得的成就，同时强调了这项运动对身体和心理的巨大挑战，以及Streeter如何学会克服恐惧并超越自我。', 'F': '找到一个让你兴奋的追求可以改善任何人的生活，但是追求激情需要纪律、努力和能力，这就是为什么它们如此有回报；对于那些开始追求新激情的人，心理学家Todd Kashdan建议他们要容忍和嘲笑自己的无知，并愿意接受负面情绪。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了医学家Mauro Zappaterra在哈佛医学院进行博士研究时的经历，他因为对治疗的好奇心与自己的研究不兼容而感到痛苦，后来他在圣菲度过的八个月中学习了哈佛没有教授的替代疗法，回来后他转换了实验室，开始研究脑脊液如何滋养发育中的神经系统，并发誓要在一切事物中寻找快乐，包括失败，因为这可以帮助他了解自己的研究和自己。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了勇气不仅仅是在身体上的表现，而是在日常生活中需要的一种品质，以及人们可以通过道德义务和努力获得勇气，并且这种努力本身也是一种令人满足的内在冒险。\n简短总结：勇气不是源于无畏，而是源于道德义务，人们可以通过努力获得勇气，并且这种努力本身也是一种令人满足的内在冒险。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
Psychologists have long held that a person's character cannot undergo a transformation in any meaningful way and that the key traits of personality are determined at a very young age. However, researchers have begun looking more closely at ways we can change. Positive psychologists have identified 24 qualities we admire, such as loyalty and kindness, and are studying them to find out why they come so naturally to some people. What they're discovering is that many of these qualities amount to habitual behaviour that determines the way we respond to the world. The good news is that all this can be learned.
Some qualities are less challenging to develop than others, optimism being one of them. However, developing qualities requires mastering a range of skills which are diverse and sometimes surprising. For example, to bring more joy and passion into your life, you must be open to experiencing negative emotions. Cultivating such qualities will help you realise your full potential.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: 'The evidence is good that most personality traits can be altered,' says Christopher Peterson, professor of psychology at the University of Michigan, who cites himself as an example. Inherently introverted, he realised early on that as an academic, his reticence would prove disastrous in the lecture hall. So he learned to be more outgoing and to entertain his classes. 'Now my extroverted behaviour is spontaneous,' he says.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: David Fajgenbaum had to make a similar transition. He was preparing for university, when he had an accident that put an end to his sports career. On campus, he quickly found that beyond ordinary counselling, the university had no services for students who were undergoing physical rehabilitation and suffering from depression like him. He therefore launched a support group to help others in similar situations. He took action despite his own pain - a typical response of an optimist.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Suzanne Segerstrom, professor of psychology at the University of Kentucky, believes that the key to increasing optimism is through cultivating optimistic behaviour, rather than positive thinking. She recommends you train yourself to pay attention to good fortune by writing down three positive things that come about each day. This will help you convince yourself that favourable outcomes actually happen all the time, making it easier to begin taking action.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: You can recognise a person who is passionate about a pursuit by the way they are so strongly involved in it. Tanya Streeter's passion is freediving – the sport of plunging deep into the water without tanks or other breathing equipment. Beginning in 1998, she set nine world records and can hold her breath for six minutes. The physical stamina required for this sport is intense but the psychological demands are even more overwhelming. Streeter learned to untangle her fears from her judgment of what her body and mind could do. 'In my career as a competitive freediver, there was a limit to what I could do - but it wasn't anywhere near what I thought it was,' she says.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Finding a pursuit that excites you can improve anyone's life. The secret about consuming passions, though, according to psychologist Paul Silvia of the University of North Carolina, is that they require discipline, hard work and ability, which is why they are so rewarding. Psychologist Todd Kashdan has this advice for those people taking up a new passion: 'As a newcomer, you also have to tolerate and laugh at your own ignorance. You must be willing to accept the negative feelings that come your way,' he says.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text:
In 2004, physician-scientist Mauro Zappaterra began his PhD research at Harvard Medical School. Unfortunately, he was miserable as his research wasn't compatible with his curiosity about healing. He finally took a break and during eight months in Santa Fe, Zappaterra learned about alternative healing techniques not taught at Harvard. When he got back, he switched labs to study how cerebrospinal fluid nourishes the developing nervous system. He also vowed to look for the joy in everything, including failure, as this could help him learn about his research and himself.
One thing that can hold joy back is a person's concentration on avoiding failure rather than their looking forward to doing something well. 'Focusing on being safe might get in the way of your reaching your goals,' explains Kashdan. For example, are you hoping to get through a business lunch without embarrassing yourself, or are you thinking about how fascinating the conversation might be?

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text:
Usually, we think of courage in physical terms but ordinary life demands something else. For marketing executive Kenneth Pedeleose, it meant speaking out against something he thought was ethically wrong. The new manager was intimidating staff so Pedeleose carefully recorded each instance of bullying and eventually took the evidence to a senior director, knowing his own job security would be threatened. Eventually the manager was the one to go. According to Cynthia Pury, a psychologist at Clemson University, Pedeleose's story proves the point that courage is not motivated by fearlessness, but by moral obligation. Pury also believes that people can acquire courage. Many of her students said that faced with a risky situation, they first tried to calm themselves down, then looked for a way to mitigate the danger, just as Pedeleose did by documenting his allegations.
Over the long term, picking up a new character trait may help you move toward being the person you want to be. And in the short term, the effort itself could be surprisingly rewarding, a kind of internal adventure.

groups.collect: Psychologists have traditionally believed that a personality $1396$was impossible and that by a $1397$a person's character tends to be fixed. target_sen.target_sen: The new manager was intimidating staff so Pedeleose carefully recorded each instance of bullying and eventually took the evidence to a senior director, knowing his own job security would be threatened.
According to Cynthia Pury, a psychologist at Clemson University, Pedeleose's story proves the point that courage is not motivated by fearlessness, but by moral obligation.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The description of any animal as an 'evolutionary throwback' is controversial. For the better part of a century, most biologists have been reluctant to use those words, mindful of a principle of evolution that says 'evolution cannot run backwards'. But as more and more examples come to light and modern genetics enters the scene, that principle is having to be rewritten. Not only are evolutionary throwbacks possible, they sometimes play an important role in the forward march of evolution.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The technical term for an evolutionary throwback is an 'atavism', from the Latin atavus, meaning forefather. The word has ugly connotations thanks largely to Cesare Lombroso, a 19th-century Italian medic who argued that criminals were born not made and could be identified by certain physical features that were throwbacks to a primitive, sub-human state.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: While Lombroso was measuring criminals, a Belgian palaeontologist called Louis Dollo was studying fossil records and coming to the opposite conclusion. In 1890 he proposed that evolution was irreversible: that 'an organism is unable to return, even partially, to a previous stage already realised in the ranks of its ancestors'. Early 20th-century biologists came to a similar conclusion, though they qualified it in terms of probability, stating that there is no reason why evolution cannot run backwards—it is just very unlikely. And so the idea of irreversibility in evolution stuck and came to be known as 'Dollo's law'.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: If Dollo's law is right, atavisms should occur only very rarely, if at all. Yet almost since the idea took root, exceptions have been cropping up. In 1919, for example, a humpback whale with a pair of leg-like appendages over a metre long, complete with a full set of limb bones, was caught off Vancouver Island in Canada. Explorer Roy Chapman Andrews argued at the time that the whale must be a throwback to a land-living ancestor. 'I can see no other explanation,' he wrote in 1921.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Since then, so many other examples have been discovered that it no longer makes sense to say that evolution is as good as irreversible. And this poses a puzzle: how can characteristics that disappeared millions of years ago suddenly reappear? In 1994, Rudolf Raff and colleagues at Indiana University in the USA decided to use genetics to put a number on the probability of evolution going into reverse. They reasoned that while some evolutionary changes involve the loss of genes and are therefore irreversible, others may be the result of genes being switched off. If these silent genes are somehow switched back on, they argued, long-lost traits could reappear.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Raff's team went on to calculate the likelihood of it happening. Silent genes accumulate random mutations, they reasoned, eventually rendering them useless. So how long can a gene survive in a species if it is no longer used? The team calculated that there is a good chance of silent genes surviving for up to 6 million years in at least a few individuals in a population, and that some might survive as long as 10 million years. In other words, throwbacks are possible, but only to the relatively recent evolutionary past.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: As a possible example, the team pointed to the mole salamanders of Mexico and California. Like most amphibians these begin life in a juvenile 'tadpole' state, then metamorphose into the adult form—except for one species, the axolotl, which famously lives its entire life as a juvenile. The simplest explanation for this is that the axolotl lineage alone lost the ability to metamorphose, while others retained it. From a detailed analysis of the salamanders' family tree, however, it is clear that the other lineages evolved from an ancestor that itself had lost the ability to metamorphose. In other words, metamorphosis in mole salamanders is an atavism. The salamander example fits with Raff's 10-million-year time frame.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: More recently, however, examples have been reported that break the time limit, suggesting that silent genes may not be the whole story. In a paper published last year, biologist Gunter Wagner of Yale University reported some work on the evolutionary history of a group of South American lizards called Bachia. Many of these have minuscule limbs; some look more like snakes than lizards and a few have completely lost the toes on their hind limbs. Other species, however, sport up to four toes on their hind legs. The simplest explanation is that the toed lineages never lost their toes, but Wagner begs to differ. According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: So what's going on? One possibility is that these traits are lost and then simply reappear, in much the same way that similar structures can independently arise in unrelated species, such as the dorsal fins of sharks and killer whales. Another more intriguing possibility is that the genetic information needed to make toes somehow survived for tens or perhaps hundreds of millions of years in the lizards and was reactivated. These atavistic traits provided an advantage and spread through the population, effectively reversing evolution.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: But if silent genes degrade within 6 to 10 million years, how can long-lost traits be reactivated over longer timescales? The answer may lie in the womb. Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features. Snake embryos, for example, sprout hind limb buds. Later in development these features disappear thanks to developmental programs that say 'lose the leg'. If for any reason this does not happen, the ancestral feature may not disappear, leading to an atavism.

questions.title: When discussing the theory developed by Louis Dollo, the writer says that

options.text: it was immediately referred to as Dollo's law.
it supported the possibility of evolutionary throwbacks.
it was modified by biologists in the early twentieth century,
it was based on many years of research.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为文章中明确提到20世纪早期的生物学家对Dollo的理论进行了修正，尽管他们认为进化逆转非常不可能，但并非完全不可能。这与题干中的“it was modified by biologists in the early twentieth century”完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Early 20th-century biologists came to a similar conclusion, though they qualified it in terms of probability, stating that there is no reason why evolution cannot run backwards- it is just very unlikely.
central_sentences.translation: 20世纪早期的生物学家得出了类似的结论，但他们从概率的角度对其进行了修正，表示没有理由认为进化不能逆转——只是这种情况非常不太可能发生。

questions.title: The humpback whale caught off Vancouver Island is mentioned because of

options.text: he exceptional size of its body.
the way it exemplifies Dollo's law.
the amount of local controversy it caused.
the reason given for its unusual features.

questions.analysis: 题干问的是为什么提到在温哥华岛捕获的座头鲸。文章中提到探险家Roy Chapman Andrews认为这只座头鲸的异常特征是返祖现象，并且他认为这是唯一的解释。这与题干中的“the reason given for its unusual features”相对应。因此，正确答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: Explorer Roy Chapman Andrews argued at the time that the whale must be a throwback to a land-living ancestor. 'I can see no other explanation,' he wrote in 1921.
central_sentences.translation: 探险家罗伊·查普曼·安德鲁斯当时认为，这头鲸鱼一定是陆地生活祖先的返祖现象。他在1921年写道："我看不出有其他解释。"

target_sen.target_sen: In 1919, for example, a humpback whale with a pair of leg-like appendages over a metre long, complete with a full set of limb bones, was caught off Vancouver Island in Canada.
In 1919, for example, a humpback whale with a pair of leg-like appendages over a metre long, complete with a full set of limb bones, was caught off Vancouver Island in Canada.

questions.title: What is said about 'silent genes'？

options.text: heir numbers vary according to species.
Raff disagreed with the use of the term.
They could lead to the re-emergence of certain characteristics.
They can have an unlimited life span.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C，因为文章中明确提到“silent genes”可以导致某些特征的重新出现。这与选项C的描述完全一致。文章中的关键句是“If these silent genes are somehow switched back on, they argued, long-lost traits could reappear.”，这句话直接对应了题干中的“silent genes”和“re-emergence”。

central_sentences.sentence: If these silent genes are somehow switched back on, they argued, long-lost traits could reappear.
central_sentences.translation: 他们认为，如果这些沉默基因以某种方式被重新激活，长期消失的特征就可能重新出现。

target_sen.target_sen: If these silent genes are somehow switched back on, they argued, long-lost traits could reappear.

questions.title: The writer mentions the mole salamander because

options.text: it exemplifies what happens in the development of most amphibians.
it suggests that Raffs theory is correct.
it has lost and regained more than one ability.
its ancestors have become the subject of extensive research.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为文章明确提到蝾螈的例子符合Raff的理论，即Raff的1000万年时间框架。这表明蝾螈的例子支持了Raff的理论。

central_sentences.sentence: The salamander example fits with Raff's 10-million-year time frame.
central_sentences.translation: 蝾螈的例子符合拉夫提出的1000万年时间框架。

syn_ary.synonym: the mole salamanders of Mexico and California

target_sen.target_sen: As a possible example, the team pointed to the mole salamanders of Mexico and California.

questions.title: Which of the following does Wagner claim?

options.text: Members of the Bachia lizard family have lost and regained certain features several times.
Evidence shows that the evolution of the Bachia lizard is due to the environment.
His research into South American lizards supports Raff's assertions.
His findings will apply to other species of South American lizards.

questions.analysis: Wagner的研究表明，Bachia蜥蜴家族的某些特征（如趾头）在数千万年间丢失并重新获得了多次。这与选项A中的描述完全一致，因此选项A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.
central_sentences.translation: 根据他对Bachia家族谱系的分析，有趾的物种是从无趾的祖先重新进化出趾头的，而且更重要的是，趾头的丢失和重新获得在数千万年间发生了不止一次。

target_sen.target_sen: The simplest explanation is that the toed lineages never lost their toes, but Wagner begs to differ.

According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-36

Complete each sentence with the correct ending，A-G, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: the question of how certain long-lost traits could reappear.
the occurrence of a particular feature in different species.
parallels drawn between behaviour and appearance.
the continued existence of certain genetic information.
the doubts felt about evolutionary throwbacks.
the possibility of evolution being reversible.
Dollo's findings and the convictions held by Lombroso.

questions.title: For a long time biologists rejected
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 F: the possibility of evolution being reversible。题干中的“biologists rejected”对应文章中的“reluctant to use”，而“evolution being reversible”对应“evolution cannot run backwards”。文章明确指出生物学家长期以来拒绝接受“进化可以倒退”的观点，因此答案是 F。

central_sentences.sentence: For the better part of a century, most biologists have been reluctant to use those words, mindful of a principle of evolution that says 'evolution cannot run backwards' . But
central_sentences.translation: 在近一个世纪的大部分时间里，大多数生物学家都不愿使用这些词，因为他们牢记一个进化原则，即"进化不能倒退"。

syn_ary.synonym: biologists have been reluctant to use those words

target_sen.target_sen: For the better part of a century, most biologists have been reluctant to use those words, mindful of a principle of evolution that says 'evolution cannot run backwards' .

questions.title: Opposing views on evolutionary throwbacks are represented by
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 G，因为文章中明确提到 Lombroso 和 Dollo 对进化倒退的不同观点。通过找到文章中的同义表达 "opposite conclusion"，可以确认他们代表了对进化倒退的不同看法。学生如果词汇量较差，可以通过关键词法、定位法和对比法来解题。容易错选的选项是 F，但它讨论的是进化是否可逆，而不是对进化倒退的不同观点。

central_sentences.sentence: While Lombroso was measuring criminals, a Belgian palaeontologist called Louis Dollo was studying fossil records and coming to the opposite conclusion.
central_sentences.translation: 当龙布罗梭在测量罪犯时，一位名叫路易斯·多洛的比利时古生物学家正在研究化石记录，并得出了相反的结论。

target_sen.target_sen: While Lombroso was measuring criminals, a Belgian palaeontologist called Louis Dollo was studying fossil records and coming to the opposite conclusion.

questions.title: Examples of evolutionary throwbacks have led to
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为题干中的“Examples of evolutionary throwbacks”在原文中对应的是“so many other examples have been discovered”，而“led to”对应的是“posed a puzzle”。原文中明确提到这些例子提出了一个问题，即“how can characteristics that disappeared millions of years ago suddenly reappear?”，这与选项A中的“the question of how certain long-lost traits could reappear”完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: And this poses a puzzle: how can characteristics that disappeared millions of years ago suddenly reappear?
central_sentences.translation: 这就提出了一个谜题：几百万年前消失的特征如何能突然重新出现？

syn_ary.synonym: characteristics that disappeared millions of years ago

target_sen.target_sen: And this poses a puzzle: how can characteristics that disappeared millions of years ago suddenly reappear?

questions.title: The shark and killer whale are mentioned to exemplify
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 B: the occurrence of a particular feature in different species。文章中提到鲨鱼和逆戟鲸的背鳍是独立出现的类似结构，这正是选项 B 所描述的“某一特定特征在不同物种中的出现”。

central_sentences.sentence: One possibility is that these traits are lost and then simply reappear, in much the same way that similar structures can independently arise in unrelated species, such as the dorsal fins of sharks and killer whales.
central_sentences.translation: 一种可能性是，这些特征先消失然后再次出现，这与相似结构在不相关的物种中独立产生的方式很相似，例如鲨鱼和虎鲸的背鳍。

target_sen.target_sen: One possibility is that these traits are lost and then simply reappear, in much the same way that similar structures can independently arise in unrelated species, such as the dorsal fins of sharks and killer whales.

questions.title: One explanation for the findings of Wagner's research is
questions.analysis: Wagner的研究发现的一个解释是某些遗传信息的继续存在并被重新激活。题干中的“Wagner's research”在原文中对应“Wagner”，而“explanation”对应“possibility”。因此，正确答案是D: the continued existence of certain genetic information。

central_sentences.sentence: Another more intriguing possibility is that the genetic information needed to make toes somehow survived for tens or perhaps hundreds of millions of years in the lizards and was reactivated.
central_sentences.translation: 另一个更有趣的可能性是，制造脚趾所需的遗传信息以某种方式在蜥蜴体内存活了数千万年甚至上亿年，并被重新激活。

target_sen.target_sen: One possibility is that these traits are lost and then simply reappear, in much the same way that similar structures can independently arise in unrelated species, such as the dorsal fins of sharks and killer whales.
Another more intriguing possibility is that the genetic information needed to make toes somehow survived for tens or perhaps hundreds of millions of years in the lizards and was reactivated.

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: Wagner was the first person to do research on South American lizards.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词包括“Wagner”、“the first person”和“research on South American lizards”。在原文中，我们找到了与这些线索词对应的表达：“Wagner”、“first”和“research on the evolutionary history of a group of South American lizards called Bachia”。然而，原文中并没有提到Wagner是第一个进行这类研究的人。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有明确给出，答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: In a paper published last year, biologist Gunter Wagner of Yale University reported some work on the evolutionary history of a group of South American lizards called Bachia.
central_sentences.translation: 在去年发表的一篇论文中，耶鲁大学的生物学家Gunter Wagner报告了一项关于一组名为Bachia的南美蜥蜴进化历史的研究。

syn_ary.synonym: biologist Gunter Wagner of Yale University

target_sen.target_sen: In a paper published last year, biologist Gunter Wagner of Yale University reported some work on the evolutionary history of a group of South American lizards called Bachia.
In a paper published last year, biologist Gunter Wagner of Yale University reported some work on the evolutionary history of a group of South American lizards called Bachia.

questions.title: Wagner believes that Bachia lizards with toes had toeless ancestors.
questions.analysis: 题干中的"Wagner believes that Bachia lizards with toes had toeless ancestors"与原文中的"According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors"相对应。原文明确指出Wagner的分析结果是有脚趾的物种是从无脚趾的祖先重新进化出脚趾的，这与题干的陈述完全一致，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.
central_sentences.translation: 根据他对Bachia家族树的分析，有脚趾的物种是从无脚趾的祖先重新进化出脚趾的，而且更重要的是，脚趾的丢失和获得在数千万年间发生了不止一次。

target_sen.target_sen: According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.

According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.

According to his analysis of the Bachia family tree, the toed species re-evolved toes from toeless ancestors and, what is more, digit loss and gain has occurred on more than one occasion over tens of millions of years.

questions.title: The temporary occurrence of long-lost traits in embryos is rare.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "The temporary occurrence of long-lost traits in embryos is rare" 意思是久远消失的特征在胚胎中暂时出现是罕见的。而原文中提到 "Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features" 表明许多物种的早期胚胎会发育出祖先特征，这意味着这种现象并不罕见。因此，题干的陈述与原文内容相矛盾，所以答案是 "NO"。

central_sentences.sentence: Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
central_sentences.translation: 许多物种的早期胚胎会发育出祖先特征。

target_sen.target_sen: Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
Later in development these features disappear thanks to developmental programs that say 'lose the leg'.
Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
Snake embryos, for example, sprout hind limb buds.
Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.

questions.title: Evolutionary throwbacks might be caused by developmental problems in the womb.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "developmental problems in the womb" 对应原文中的 "developmental programs" 和 "embryos"。原文提到，如果发育程序没有按预期进行，祖先特征可能不会消失，从而导致返祖现象。这与题干中的观点一致，因此答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features. Snake embryos, for example, sprout hind limb buds. Later in development these features disappear thanks to developmental programs that say 'lose the leg'. If for any reason this does not happen, the ancestral feature may not disappear, leading to an atavism.
central_sentences.translation: 许多物种的早期胚胎会发展出祖先特征。例如，蛇的胚胎会长出后肢芽。在后期发育过程中，这些特征会因发育程序的指令（如"失去腿部"）而消失。如果由于某些原因这个过程没有发生，祖先特征可能不会消失，从而导致返祖现象。

target_sen.target_sen: Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
Later in development these features disappear thanks to developmental programs that say 'lose the leg'.
Early embryos of many species develop ancestral features.
Snake embryos, for example, sprout hind limb buds.
